title: Delays with automations
excerpt:
Hello. I’m experiencing significant delays when running automations. 
For example, I have an automation "When Status changes set Another Status to some value’. And when I change the Status column, it takes 5-10+ seconds …

posts.author: NodarDavituri (Nodar Davituri)
posts.cookedHtml:
Hello. I’m experiencing significant delays when running automations.

For example, I have an automation "When Status changes set Another Status to some value’. And when I change the Status column, it takes 5-10+ seconds for this automation to run and to set the Another Status value. Sometimes, the Another Status value never changes until I manually refresh the browser tab.

Same goes for other change-triggered automations (moving to the group, setting dates, etc).

Is that a general/known problem?

P.S. also a quick question about date automation: how can I set the relative date to the date column? For example, setting the date column value to “7 days from now”. The only option the “set date” action provides is “Today”. Right now I’m doing 2 actions for this - “set date” which sets Today’s date and “push date” which pushes the current date +7 days in future. This works but feels hack-y. Also, I get the double delay with this approach - when this automation triggers, it sets the column value to the current date and it gets pushed to +7 days only 5+ seconds later…

Thanks!

posts.author: basdebruin (Bas de Bruin)
posts.cookedHtml:
hi @NodarDavituri

I too experienced some issues with monday (automations and integrations). At that time the monday status page https://status.monday.com/ also was showing connectivity issues. It looks much better now.

posts.author: nu1 (Nuwan Samarasinghe)
posts.cookedHtml:
Hi @NodarDavituri,

I recently discovered that automations work best if you create them from scratch on the board. If you copied them over from a prior board, that sometimes introduces delays in execution.

We were testing some functionality in our Sandbox, then copied them over to our Production board, only to have them slow down or not run at all. Turns out that deleting the copied automation and rebuilding from scratch usually solves the problem. Hope this helps.

Also - +1 vote for the ability to set a Relative Date - e.g. “7 days from now” in Date automations.
